2605.02335.title: LLM-enabled Social Agents
2605.02335.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have transformed agent-agent and human-agent interaction by enabling software, physical, and simulation agents to communicate and deliberate through natural language. Yet fluent language use does not by itself yield socially intelligible behaviour. Most current systems remain weakly grounded in roles, norms, intentions, and contextual constraints, limiting their capacity for meaningful participation in social environments. This paper develops a conceptual baseline for LLM-enabled social agents by arguing that they should be grounded in role definitions operationalized through persona descriptions. On this basis, we outline research directions for representation, hybrid control, and evaluation. The paper concludes that persona-based role definitions are a necessary foundation for turning language competence into social behaviour.
2605.02335.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）通過使軟體、實體和模擬代理能夠通過自然語言進行交流和討論，改變了代理之間以及人類與代理之間的互動。然而，流利的語言使用本身並不會產生社會上可理解的行為。大多數當前系統在角色、規範、意圖和上下文限制方面仍然基礎薄弱，限制了它們在社會環境中有意義參與的能力。本文通過主張社會代理應該基於通過角色描述具體化的角色定義，為LLM支持的社會代理開發了一個概念基線。在此基礎上，我們概述了代表性、混合控制和評估的研究方向。本文結論認為，基於角色的角色定義是將語言能力轉化為社會行為的必要基礎。
2605.02335.author: Önder Gürcan et.al.
